description: Conformance vectors for verdict_ref, an optional envelope-adjacent field binding action_ref to an independent pre- or post-action judgment. verdict_ref does not enter the action_ref preimage (four fields only, per action-ref.md §3.1) — same envelope-adjacent pattern as authority_verified_at_ms / revocation_check_at_ms. Conformance gates on issuer independence (issuer_id != agent_id), not on the verdict outcome itself.

vectors.description: No verdict_ref present. This is action_ref's default and only mode today: authorization_ref / freshness fields (action-ref.md §4.2) prove the delegation chain was valid at admission, but nothing in the base envelope carries a third party's judgment on this specific instance. Self-attested by construction — not a defect, the documented baseline this fixture set extends.

verdict_ref present, issuer_id distinct from the executing agent_id, verdict=approve. Conformant: independence holds, so the citation in scope (or an envelope-adjacent verdict_ref field) is a genuine third-party judgment, not the agent grading itself.

verdict_ref present but issuer_id equals the executing agent_id — the agent judging its own action and citing the judgment as if it were third-party. Non-conformant: a verifier MUST reject this as an independent verdict, even though the hash recomputes correctly. Hash validity and independence are separate checks; this vector fails the second one.

verdict_ref present, issuer_id distinct, verdict=reject. Conformant: independence is what gates conformance, not the verdict outcome — a reject from an independent issuer is exactly as valid a citation as an approve. A scheme that only accepts independent verdict_ref on 'approve' would let the agent selectively suppress unfavorable third-party judgments.

notes: verdict_ref = SHA-256(JCS(verdict_object)), where verdict_object = {action_ref, confidence, issuer_id, ts_ms, verdict}. Envelope-adjacent per action-ref.md §3.1: it never enters the four-field action_ref preimage (agent_id, action_type, scope, timestamp).

independence invariant: verdict.issuer_id != preimage.agent_id. This is a semantic check on top of hash recomputation — vr-003 recomputes correctly and still fails conformance, same pattern as composition-ref-v1's non-conformant-but-hash-valid vectors.

verdict_ref binds forward to the action_ref it judges (verdict.action_ref), so a verifier confirms both directions: recompute action_ref from the preimage, recompute verdict_ref from the verdict object, and confirm verdict.action_ref matches the action_ref in scope.

This complements, not replaces, agentoracle-v1 (examples/conformance/agentoracle-v1/): that fixture set shows a pre-action gate receipt embedding action_ref as a forward pointer (verdict receipt -> subject action). verdict-ref-v1 is the reverse binding (action envelope -> citing the verdict that judged it) — both directions are now conformance-covered.
